turns.content: It is APRIL 15th. April. And it was minus twenty this morning. I am going to personally fight this city's weather
Minus twenty in mid-April is brutal even by Montreal standards. Is winter just refusing to end this year?

It's been like this all week. Last week it was plus five and I could see the sidewalks for the first time since November. I thought okay, spring is coming, we survived. And then this morning I walked outside and my face hurt within thirty seconds. My FACE. The air attacked my face
Montreal has a special talent for giving you one week of hope and then snatching it away. Are you still biking to work in this, or did you switch to the metro?

I biked. I always bike. I'm stubborn and possibly stupid. I have this whole system — two layers of merino wool, a balaclava, ski goggles, lobster gloves. I look like I'm going to rob a bank on my way to the restaurant. But my fingers were still numb by the time I got to the kitchen
Numb fingers are a real problem for someone who needs fine motor control for work. How long is your commute by bike?

About twenty minutes normally, but in winter with the ice it's more like thirty. The bike lanes are garbage — the city plows the roads but the bike lanes get whatever's left, which is basically a layer of ice with some sand thrown on top as a gesture of politeness. I've wiped out twice this winter. Once I slid into a snowbank on Saint-Denis and just lay there for a minute contemplating my life choices
Lying in a snowbank contemplating your life choices is peak Montreal winter cycling. What keeps you biking instead of just taking the metro? Stubbornness alone, or is there more to it?

Stubbornness is like 60% of it. But also — after a night of service, getting on the bike at midnight and riding home in the cold is the only thing that clears my head. The kitchen is so hot and loud and intense, and then you step outside and it's minus fifteen and silent and the cold just... resets your brain. It's like a hard reboot. I need that transition or I can't sleep
That makes a lot of sense — the temperature shock as a mental decompression tool. The ride home in the cold after a hot kitchen is almost like a ritual. Do you at least have winter tires on the bike?

Studded tires, oui. Schwalbe Marathon Winters. They're the only reason I'm alive. Without studs on Montreal ice you might as well just throw yourself directly into traffic and save time. But even with studs, black ice is black ice. You can't stud your way out of physics

"You can't stud your way out of physics" should be printed on every Montreal winter cycling brochure. At least the worst of it should be over soon — even Montreal can't keep this up past May. Usually.

You say that but last year it snowed in May. MAY. This city is unhinged. But fine, I'll survive. I survived St. Petersburg winters as a child, I can survive this. At least here the metro works when I'm too pathetic to bike. Okay I'm going to go make tea and thaw my feet. Merde, April
Go thaw. Spring will come eventually — Montreal just makes you earn it more than most cities. Stay upright out there.
